message:
test(drivers): add unit tests for driver interface layer (bd-t3mp)

Add 13 unit tests covering:
- detect_review_driver: auto-detect available driver (ntm > local > none)
- load_review_driver: load driver functions
- driver_capabilities: query driver JSON capabilities
- Unified driver_* interface after loading local driver

Note: Functions with heredocs (_enable_local_driver, local_driver_get_session_state)
are defined inline since source_ru_function sed extraction can't handle heredocs.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-cuq5","title":"Phase 5: Apply Phase (Quality Gates, gh_actions, Push)","description":"Phase 5: Apply Phase

Overview
--------
Execute approved actions from review plans: run quality gates, perform
GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels), and push changes.

Why Separate Phase?
-------------------
- Plan mode is safe (no mutations)
- User reviews plans before execution
- Quality gates catch issues before push
- Explicit --apply flag required
- Auditability of what was done

Components
----------

5.1 Quality Gates Framework
   Run tests/lint before allowing push:
   - Per-repo policy configuration
   - Auto-detect test commands
   - Fail blocks push
   - Secret scanning

5.2 Per-Repo Policy Configuration
   ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:
   - REVIEW_TEST_CMD
   - REVIEW_LINT_CMD
   - REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS
   - Custom validation scripts

5.3 gh_actions Execution
   Execute actions from review-plan.json:
   - comment: gh issue comment / gh pr comment
   - close: gh issue close / gh pr close
   - label: gh issue edit --add-label
   - (merge not supported - policy says no PRs)

5.4 Worktree Merge and Push
   Merge worktree changes to main:
   - Verify quality gates passed
   - Merge worktree branch to base
   - Push to remote
   - Clean up worktree

5.5 Apply Progress Tracking
   Track what has been applied:
   - Mark items as applied in state
   - Log all gh_actions executed
   - Record push timestamps
   - Enable partial retry

5.6 Rollback Capability
   If something goes wrong:
   - Worktrees preserved until explicit cleanup
   - Can revert commits locally
   - gh_actions not reversible (comments stay)
   - Plan artifact serves as audit log

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Quality gates run before push
- gh_actions execute correctly
- Push only with explicit --push flag
- State tracks what was applied
- Rollback path documented

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines of Bash","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:39:37.725040272-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:35:52.053436297-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:35:52.053436297-05:00","close_reason":"All components implemented:

- 5.5 Apply Progress Tracking: record_gh_action_log() with JSONL at line 11989
- 5.6 Rollback: Worktrees preserved, plan artifacts serve as audit log (per design)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cuq5","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:26.708977103-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-cutq","title":"Implement per-repo review policy configuration","description":"# Task: Implement Per-Repo Review Policy Configuration

## Purpose
Allow users to customize review behavior per repository through configuration files. Different repos have different test commands, lint requirements, and review policies.

## Background
Not all repos are the same:
- Some have Makefile, others use npm
- Some require strict lint, others are more lenient
- Some repos should never auto-push
- Some repos need specific branch protection rules

## Configuration Structure

### Directory Layout
```
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/
├── _default.conf          # Default policy (applies to all)
├── owner_repo1.conf       # Policy for owner/repo1
├── owner_repo2.conf       # Policy for owner/repo2
└── team_*.conf            # Glob patterns work too
```

### Policy File Format
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/myorg_backend.conf

# Test configuration
REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300          # seconds

# Lint configuration
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=true        # Block push if lint fails

# Secret scanning
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_SECRET_PATTERNS="AWS_|PRIVATE_KEY|password"

# Push policy
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # false = never push for this repo
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # true = always ask before push

# Priority overrides
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=50          # Add to all items from this repo
REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST="urgent:30,security:40"

# Review behavior
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=10              # Max items to review per session
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false            # true = only review issues
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false           # true = comprehensive review
```

### Default Policy
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/_default.conf

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
```

## Implementation

### load_repo_policy()
```bash
load_repo_policy() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    # Reset to defaults first
    source_default_policy
    
    # Try exact match
    local exact_file="$policy_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
    if [[ -f "$exact_file" ]]; then
        source "$exact_file"
        log_verbose "Loaded policy: $exact_file"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Try glob patterns (owner_*.conf, team_*.conf)
    local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
    for pattern_file in "$policy_dir"/"${owner}"_*.conf "$policy_dir"/*_*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$pattern_file" ]]; then
            # Check if pattern matches
            local pattern_base
            pattern_base=$(basename "$pattern_file" .conf)
            if [[ "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/\/} || \
                  "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/}* ]]; then
                source "$pattern_file"
                log_verbose "Loaded policy via pattern: $pattern_file"
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    log_verbose "Using default policy for $repo_id"
    return 0
}
```

### source_default_policy()
```bash
source_default_policy() {
    local default_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/_default.conf"
    
    # Built-in defaults
    REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""
    REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300
    REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
    REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
    REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
    REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
    REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
    REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
    REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST=""
    REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
    REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
    REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
    
    # Override with user defaults if present
    [[ -f "$default_file" ]] && source "$default_file"
}
```

### apply_policy_priority_boost()
```bash
apply_policy_priority_boost() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local base_score="$2"
    local labels="$3"
    
    load_repo_policy "$repo_id"
    
    local score=$base_score
    
    # Add base priority boost
    score=$((score + REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY))
    
    # Apply label boosts
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST" ]]; then
        IFS="," read -ra boosts <<< "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST"
        for boost in "${boosts[@]}"; do
            local label="${boost%%:*}"
            local points="${boost##*:}"
            if echo "$labels" | grep -qi "$label"; then
                score=$((score + points))
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    echo "$score"
}
```

### validate_policy_file()
```bash
validate_policy_file() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check syntax
    if ! bash -n "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_error "Syntax error in policy file: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check for required variables
    source "$file"
    
    # Validate values
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" ]] && ! [[ "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT in $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

### init_review_policies()
```bash
init_review_policies() {
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$policy_dir" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$policy_dir"
        
        # Create example default policy
        cat > "$policy_dir/_default.conf.example" << "EOF"
# Default review policy (rename to _default.conf to activate)
# These settings apply to all repos unless overridden

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect (Makefile, package.json, etc.)
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""               # Optional lint command
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true          # Scan for secrets before push
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # Allow pushing changes
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # Require confirmation before push
EOF
        log_info "Created example policy file: $policy_dir/_default.conf.example"
    fi
}
```

## Integration Points
- Called by priority scoring (apply_policy_priority_boost)
- Called by quality gates (load test/lint commands)
- Called by push phase (check REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH)
- Called during cmd_review init

## Testing
- Verify default policy applied
- Verify exact match policy overrides default
- Verify glob pattern matching works
- Verify invalid policy files rejected
- Verify priority boosts applied correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default policy applied to all repos
- [ ] Per-repo policies override defaults
- [ ] Glob patterns work for org-wide settings
- [ ] Policy validation catches errors
- [ ] Example policy created on init

 {"id":"bd-sv6v","title":"Implement retry with exponential backoff","description":"Retry with exponential backoff for transient failures.

Function: retry_with_backoff(cmd, max_attempts, base_delay)

Algorithm:
- Start with base_delay (default 1s)
- On failure: delay = base * 2^(attempt-1)
- Add jitter: +/- 25% randomization
- Continue until success or max_attempts

Use cases:
- API calls that timeout
- Rate limit recovery
- Network glitches

Implementation logs warnings on each retry, returns final exit code.

Acceptance: Works for common failure patterns, respects max attempts, jitter prevents thundering herd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:23.01899538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","close_reason":"Added retry_with_backoff (exp backoff + jitter) and used for gh api GraphQL/rate_limit; fixed governor grep portability","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv6v","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:15.633158875-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-tsya","title":"Fix test_unit_review cleanup + exit-code assertions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T23:31:19.377435171-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:33:08.777160299-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:33:08.777160299-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: safer mock isolation + assert exit codes"}
 {"id":"bd-vcr9","title":"Implement gh_actions execution from review plan","description":"Task: Implement gh_actions Execution

Purpose
-------
Execute GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels) from the gh_actions
array in review-plan.json during the Apply phase.

gh_actions Schema
-----------------
{
  "gh_actions": [
    {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "body": "Fixed in..."},
    {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "reason": "completed"},
    {"op": "label", "target": "issue#42", "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]},
    {"op": "comment", "target": "pr#15", "body": "Thank you..."}
  ]
}

Supported Operations
--------------------

1. comment
   Add comment to issue or PR
   gh issue comment N --body "..."
   gh pr comment N --body "..."

2. close
   Close issue or PR
   gh issue close N --reason "..."
   gh pr close N --comment "..."

3. label
   Add labels to issue
   gh issue edit N --add-label "label1,label2"

4. (merge - NOT SUPPORTED per policy)

Implementation
--------------

execute_gh_actions()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  local actions
  actions=$(jq -c .gh_actions[] "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  
  while IFS= read -r action; do
    local op target
    op=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .op)
    target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
    
    case "$op" in
      comment) execute_comment "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      close)   execute_close "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      label)   execute_label "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      *)
        log_warn "Unknown gh_action op: $op"
        ;;
    esac
  done <<< "$actions"

execute_comment()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target body type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  body=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .body)
  
  # Parse target: "issue#42" or "pr#15"
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Commenting on $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_close()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target reason type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  reason=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".reason // \"completed\"")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Closing $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --reason "$reason"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --comment "Closing: $reason"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_label()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target labels_json type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  labels_json=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".labels | join(\",\")")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Adding labels to $target: $labels_json"
  
  gh issue edit "$number" -R "$repo_id" --add-label "$labels_json"
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

Action Recording
----------------
Track what was executed for audit:

record_action_executed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/gh_actions.log"
  local ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
  
  echo "${ts}|${repo_id}|${action}" >> "$log_file"

Error Handling
--------------
- Log errors but continue with other actions
- Record failed actions in state
- Retry logic for transient failures
- Skip already-executed actions on resume

Testing
-------
- Mock gh commands for unit tests
- Verify comment body escaping
- Verify close reasons work
- Verify labels applied
- Test error handling

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Comments posted correctly
- [ ] Issues/PRs closed with reason
- [ ] Labels applied
- [ ] Actions logged for audit
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent on retry","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:09.158053493-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented gh_actions execution with 9 functions: execute_gh_actions, execute_gh_action_{comment,close,label}, gh_action_already_executed, record_gh_action_log, parse_gh_action_target, canonicalize_gh_action, get_gh_actions_log_file. Features idempotence, audit logging, policy enforcement.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vcr9","depends_on_id":"bd-ldyv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.518493177-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,443 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# Unit tests: Driver interface layer (bd-t3mp)
+#
+# Tests the unified driver interface that provides abstraction over
+# different session drivers (local tmux, ntm).
+#
+# Functions tested:
+# - detect_review_driver(): Auto-detect available driver
+# - load_review_driver(): Load driver functions
+# - driver_capabilities(): Query driver capabilities
+# - driver_* unified interface: Verify local driver integration
+#
+# shellcheck disable=SC1091  # Sourced files checked separately
+# shellcheck disable=SC2317  # Test functions invoked indirectly via run_test
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+
+source "$SCRIPT_DIR/test_framework.sh"
+
+# Skip if tmux unavailable (needed for local driver)
+if ! command -v tmux &>/dev/null; then
+    echo "SKIP: tmux not available, skipping driver interface tests"
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+# Source required functions from ru
+source_ru_function "detect_review_driver"
+source_ru_function "load_review_driver"
+
+# Define _enable_local_driver inline (redefines driver_* functions)
+# This is needed because the function redefines other functions which
+# breaks when extracted with sed
+_enable_local_driver() {
+    driver_start_session() {
+        local_driver_start_session "$@"
+    }
+    driver_send_to_session() {
+        local_driver_send_to_session "$@"
+    }
+    driver_get_session_state() {
+        local_driver_get_session_state "$@"
+    }
+    driver_stop_session() {
+        local_driver_stop_session "$@"
+    }
+    driver_interrupt_session() {
+        local_driver_interrupt_session "$@"
+    }
+    driver_stream_events() {
+        local_driver_stream_events "$@"
+    }
+    driver_list_sessions() {
+        local_driver_list_sessions "$@"
+    }
+    driver_session_alive() {
+        local_driver_session_alive "$@"
+    }
+
+    # Update capabilities for local driver
+    driver_capabilities() {
+        cat <<EOF
+{
+  "name": "local",
+  "parallel_sessions": true,
+  "activity_detection": false,
+  "health_monitoring": false,
+  "question_routing": true,
+  "max_concurrent": 4
+}
+EOF
+    }
+}
+
+# Source local driver functions (needed by _enable_local_driver)
+source_ru_function "local_driver_session_alive"
+source_ru_function "local_driver_stop_session"
+source_ru_function "local_driver_list_sessions"
+source_ru_function "local_driver_send_to_session"
+source_ru_function "local_driver_interrupt_session"
+
+# Define local_driver_get_session_state inline (has heredocs)
+local_driver_get_session_state() {
+    local session_id="$1"
+
+    if ! tmux has-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null; then
+        cat <<EOF
+{
+  "session_id": "$session_id",
+  "state": "dead",
+  "reason": "tmux session not found"
+}
+EOF
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local pane_pid
+    pane_pid=$(tmux list-panes -t "$session_id" -F "#{pane_pid}" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
+
+    if [[ -z "$pane_pid" ]]; then
+        cat <<EOF
+{
+  "session_id": "$session_id",
+  "state": "unknown",
+  "reason": "no pane found"
+}
+EOF
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local children
+    children=$(pgrep -P "$pane_pid" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
+
+    local state="generating"
+    if [[ "$children" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        state="complete"
+    fi
+
+    cat <<EOF
+{
+  "session_id": "$session_id",
+  "state": "$state",
+  "pane_pid": $pane_pid
+}
+EOF
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Global for driver loading
+LOADED_DRIVER=""
+
+# Stubs for logging
+log_error() { echo "ERROR: $*" >&2; }
+log_verbose() { :; }
+
+# Test session prefix
+TEST_SESSION_PREFIX="ru-test-iface-$$"
+
+# Helper: Create a simple test tmux session
+create_test_session() {
+    local session_name="$1"
+    local command="${2:-sleep 300}"
+    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" "$command" 2>/dev/null
+}
+
+# Helper: Kill test session
+cleanup_test_session() {
+    local session_name="$1"
+    tmux kill-session -t "$session_name" 2>/dev/null || true
+}
+
+# Cleanup all test sessions on exit
+cleanup_all_test_sessions() {
+    local sessions
+    sessions=$(tmux list-sessions -F "#{session_name}" 2>/dev/null | grep "^${TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}" || true)
+    for session in $sessions; do
+        tmux kill-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null || true
+    done
+}
+
+trap cleanup_all_test_sessions EXIT
+
+#==============================================================================
+# Tests: detect_review_driver
+#==============================================================================
+
+test_detect_driver_finds_local_when_tmux_available() {
+    local test_name="detect_review_driver: returns 'local' when tmux available"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    # tmux is available (we checked at top of script)
+    local driver
+    driver=$(detect_review_driver)
+
+    # Should return either "ntm" or "local" - not "none"
+    if [[ "$driver" == "local" || "$driver" == "ntm" ]]; then
+        pass "detects available driver"
+    else
+        fail "detects available driver" "local or ntm" "$driver"
+    fi
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_detect_driver_returns_none_without_tools() {
+    local test_name="detect_review_driver: returns 'none' without tools"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    # Save PATH and create empty PATH
+    local old_path="$PATH"
+    export PATH="/nonexistent"
+
+    local driver
+    driver=$(detect_review_driver)
+
+    export PATH="$old_path"
+
+    assert_equals "none" "$driver" "returns none without tmux or ntm"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+#==============================================================================
+# Tests: load_review_driver
+#==============================================================================
+
+test_load_driver_local_succeeds() {
+    local test_name="load_review_driver: loads local driver successfully"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    LOADED_DRIVER=""
+
+    local rc=0
+    load_review_driver "local" || rc=$?
+
+    assert_equals "0" "$rc" "returns 0 for local driver"
+    assert_equals "local" "$LOADED_DRIVER" "LOADED_DRIVER set to local"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_load_driver_none_fails() {
+    local test_name="load_review_driver: fails for 'none' driver"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local rc=0
+    load_review_driver "none" 2>/dev/null || rc=$?
+
+    assert_equals "1" "$rc" "returns 1 for none driver"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_load_driver_unknown_fails() {
+    local test_name="load_review_driver: fails for unknown driver"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local rc=0
+    load_review_driver "unknown-driver" 2>/dev/null || rc=$?
+
+    assert_equals "1" "$rc" "returns 1 for unknown driver"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+#==============================================================================
+# Tests: driver_capabilities
+#==============================================================================
+
+test_driver_capabilities_returns_json() {
+    local test_name="driver_capabilities: returns valid JSON"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    # Load local driver first
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local caps
+    caps=$(driver_capabilities)
+
+    # Check it's valid JSON
+    if echo "$caps" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
+        pass "returns valid JSON"
+    else
+        fail "returns valid JSON" "valid JSON" "invalid JSON"
+    fi
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_driver_capabilities_has_name() {
+    local test_name="driver_capabilities: includes driver name"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local caps
+    caps=$(driver_capabilities)
+
+    local name
+    name=$(echo "$caps" | jq -r '.name' 2>/dev/null)
+
+    assert_equals "local" "$name" "name is 'local'"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_driver_capabilities_has_parallel_sessions() {
+    local test_name="driver_capabilities: includes parallel_sessions"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local caps
+    caps=$(driver_capabilities)
+
+    local parallel
+    parallel=$(echo "$caps" | jq -r '.parallel_sessions' 2>/dev/null)
+
+    assert_equals "true" "$parallel" "parallel_sessions is true"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+#==============================================================================
+# Tests: Unified interface after loading local driver
+#==============================================================================
+
+test_unified_session_alive_works() {
+    local test_name="driver_session_alive: works after loading local driver"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local session="${TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}-alive"
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+
+    create_test_session "$session"
+
+    if driver_session_alive "$session"; then
+        pass "driver_session_alive returns true for existing"
+    else
+        fail "driver_session_alive returns true for existing" "0" "non-zero"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_unified_stop_session_works() {
+    local test_name="driver_stop_session: works after loading local driver"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local session="${TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}-stop"
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+
+    create_test_session "$session"
+
+    driver_stop_session "$session"
+
+    if tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null; then
+        fail "driver_stop_session kills session" "no session" "session exists"
+    else
+        pass "driver_stop_session kills session"
+    fi
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_unified_get_state_works() {
+    local test_name="driver_get_session_state: works after loading local driver"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local session="${TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}-state"
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+
+    # Test non-existent session
+    local state
+    state=$(driver_get_session_state "$session")
+
+    local state_value
+    state_value=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.state' 2>/dev/null)
+
+    assert_equals "dead" "$state_value" "returns dead for nonexistent"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_unified_list_sessions_works() {
+    local test_name="driver_list_sessions: works after loading local driver"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local session="ru-${TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}-list"
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+
+    create_test_session "$session"
+
+    local sessions
+    sessions=$(driver_list_sessions)
+
+    assert_contains "$sessions" "$session" "lists ru- prefixed session"
+
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_unified_send_to_session_works() {
+    local test_name="driver_send_to_session: works after loading local driver"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    load_review_driver "local"
+
+    local session="${TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}-send"
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+
+    create_test_session "$session" "cat"
+    sleep 0.2
+
+    local rc=0
+    driver_send_to_session "$session" "test" || rc=$?
+
+    assert_equals "0" "$rc" "sends to existing session"
+
+    cleanup_test_session "$session"
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+#==============================================================================
+# Run Tests
+#==============================================================================
+
+log_suite_start "Unit Tests: Driver Interface Layer"
+
+# detect_review_driver tests
+run_test test_detect_driver_finds_local_when_tmux_available
+run_test test_detect_driver_returns_none_without_tools
+
+# load_review_driver tests
+run_test test_load_driver_local_succeeds
+run_test test_load_driver_none_fails
+run_test test_load_driver_unknown_fails
+
+# driver_capabilities tests
+run_test test_driver_capabilities_returns_json
+run_test test_driver_capabilities_has_name
+run_test test_driver_capabilities_has_parallel_sessions
+
+# Unified interface tests
+run_test test_unified_session_alive_works
+run_test test_unified_stop_session_works
+run_test test_unified_get_state_works
+run_test test_unified_list_sessions_works
+run_test test_unified_send_to_session_works
+
+print_results
+exit $?
